verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase F1, a soluble catalytic protein resident in the mitochondrial matrix. The F1 core is a multi-subunit complex of the Fo-F1 ATP synthase embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane; the F1 portion itself is not membrane-anchored and resides on the matrix side of the inner membrane. No surface topology, TM domain, GPI anchor, lipidation, or stable complex-partner anchoring to the plasma membrane is documented. The protein is not a shedding target, does not traffic through secretory pathways, and has no known cancer-ecto or cell-death-induced surface display. The canonical role is strictly mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation. DYT38 (dystonia-38) association reflects a neuropathological mitochondrial phenotype, not surface biology.
